content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 22, 1994

Kevin Barnes
Refuse Superintendent
City of San Bernardino
300 North "D" Street
San Bernardino, CA 92418

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-94-028

Dear Mr. Barnes:

We have received your letter requesting clarification of
advice you received from this office. You are not seeking advice
concerning a specific governmental decision before you at this
time. Therefore your letter is considered a request for informal
assistance.1/ You were previously advised that if you purchase
refuse collection equipment from Amrep, Inc., at the fair market
value, without receiving a discount from Amrep, Inc., you would
not have an economic interest in Amrep requiring you to disqualify
yourself from participating in decisions.

You are now asking if you are permitted to pay Amrep, Inc.,
to manufacture the refuse collection equipment exclusively for
your resale.

The advice rendered by the Fair Political Practices
Commission under the Political Reform Act (the "Act") is limited
to an individual's responsibilities when participating in
governmental decisions as a public official. It is not within the
realm of our authority to advise you of your duties in your
private capacity. Payments by you to Amrep, Inc., to manufacture
equipment exclusively for your resale, do not create an economic
interest in Amrep, Inc. Consequently, this would not require you
to disqualify yourself from participating in subsequent city
decisions affecting Amrep, Inc.

1/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Advice File No. I-94-028
Page 2


     Other conflict of interest laws, such as Government Code
Section 1090, may affect you.  The Commission has no jurisdiction
to interpret or enforce Section 1090.  Any questions should be
addressed to the Attorney General or your city attorney.

     You are also asking if you could sell your equipment to
haulers who are not in the City of San Bernardino.

     You were also advised, in that previous advice, that you
could not, in your official capacity, participate in any decision
which would have a material financial effect on any source of
income, which would include haulers who purchased equipment from
you.  (Barnes Advice Letter, No. I-93-451.)  Therefore, there is
nothing preventing you from selling equipment to haulers outside
the City of San Bernardino.  However, if any of the haulers are
sources of income to you in excess of $250 during the 12-month
period before a city decision which will have a material financial
effect on that hauler, you cannot participate in that decision.

     If you have any additional questions concerning your duties
under the Act, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                              Sincerely,

                              Steven G. Churchwell
                              General Counsel

                              By:  Jeanette E. Turvill
                                   Political Reform Consultant
                                   Fair Political Practices Commission

SGC/JET/jt

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a public official can pay a private company to manufacture refuse collection equipment exclusively for resale without creating an economic interest, and whether the official can sell such equipment to haulers outside the city.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Payments to manufacture equipment exclusively for resale do not create an economic interest in the manufacturer requiring disqualification from city decisions affecting that company. The official may sell equipment to haulers outside the city, but cannot participate in city decisions materially affecting any hauler who is a source of income exceeding $250 in the 12 months prior to the decision.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but legal questions and answers are clearly identifiable in body text. File references previous advice letter No. 1-93-451. Notes that informal assistance does not provide immunity of formal written advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a public official can pay a private company to manufacture refuse collection equipment exclusively for resale without creating an economic interest, and whether the official can sell such equipment to haulers outside the city.

CONCLUSION: Payments to manufacture equipment exclusively for resale do not create an economic interest in the manufacturer requiring disqualification from city decisions affecting that company. The official may sell equipment to haulers outside the city, but cannot participate in city decisions materially affecting any hauler who is a source of income exceeding $250 in the 12 months prior to the decision.

You were also advised, in that previous advice, that you could not, in your official capacity, participate in any decision which would have a material financial effect on any source of income, which would include haulers who purchased equipment from you. Turvill Political Reform Consultant Fair Political Practices Commission SGC/JET/jt
embedding.summary: FPPC responds to informal assistance request from a city refuse superintendent clarifying that manufacturing equipment for resale does not create an economic interest in the manufacturer under the Political Reform Act, and that selling equipment to out-of-city haulers is permitted subject to disqualification rules for decisions affecting those haulers as income sources.
